issue.body:
## Backfill

Retroactive feature-request issue for spec #29 (glyph-level annotations).
Filed as part of R2 Phase B to close the chain-backfill gap workspace-rc
left open.

## Feature

Add a parallel annotation structure to `Word` that records glyph-level
facts about the printed page (ligatures, long-s substitutions, swash forms,
etc.) **without** mutating the canonical / semantic ground-truth string.

This is the **foundation-library half** of a workspace-wide feature needed
by `pd-ocr-synth`, `pd-ocr-trainer`, `pd-ocr-labeler`, and
`pd-ocr-labeler-spa`.

## Spec

See #29 / `pd-book-tools/docs/specs/05-glyph-annotations.md`.

## Triage status

Pre-approved by virtue of having a written spec. Skipping /triage; the spec
issue (#29) is the canonical child.

Spec: docs/specs/05-glyph-annotations.md
issue.title: Glyph-Level Side-Channel Annotations on Word

issue.title: Spec: Glyph-Level Side-Channel Annotations on `Word`

issue.body:
Discovered while adding the `source` provenance field (commit e0fb16d).

Two divergences from pd-ocr-labeler-spa `specs/20-glyph-annotations.md` remain unresolved:

1. **LigatureKind** — upstream values are lowercase with a different member set (has FF/LONG_S_S/LONG_S_I/SP/QU, lacks OE/AE, names LONG_S_T); the SPA spec wants UPPERCASE values, drops/adds members, and renames to LONG_ST. Changing enum values is a wire-format break for #41-era persisted snapshots — needs a migration plan.
2. **dataclass vs Pydantic** — the upstream model is a `@dataclass`; the SPA spec writes it as a Pydantic `BaseModel`. Decide whether the SPA adapts or upstream converts.

Blocks full completion of the pd-ocr-labeler-spa glyph epic (#267-#270).
issue.title: Reconcile GlyphAnnotations model with pd-ocr-labeler-spa spec 20 (LigatureKind vocab, Pydantic)
